is_p_falciparum.evidence: Study analyzes P. falciparum dhfr/dhps and other resistance markers in clinical malaria episodes.

reports_target_markers.evidence: Reports dhfr-51,59,108; dhps-437,540; pfcrt-76; pfmdr1-86 — all curated positions.

reports_extractable_frequencies.evidence: Tables 1 and 2 give No. (%) of patients with wild type/mutant/mixed for each codon with denominators (e.g., dhps-437 mutant 43(61) of n=70).

sub_country_location.evidence: Conducted in Manhiça, southern Mozambique — a single sub-national site (district), so frequencies tie below country level.

temporal_resolution_within_3y.evidence: Study nested in an IPTi trial with infants dosed at 3,4,9 months and follow-up to 24 months. No explicit calendar collection years are given, and samples span the full trial follow-up period; cannot confirm the sampling window is within 3 years or bin frequencies temporally.

african_field_samples.evidence: Human clinical isolates (filter paper blood samples from microscopically confirmed malaria episodes) collected in Manhiça, Mozambique.

is_primary_study.evidence: Primary empirical molecular analysis of samples from a randomized IPTi trial.

duplicate_risk.evidence: Samples are from the IPTi trial cohort [ref 4, Macete et al. 2006, J Infect Dis 194:276–85]; 'This study was performed in the context of a randomized, double-blind, placebo-controlled trial of IPTi with SP' and 'a parallel study of this same cohort of children.' The parent trial and parallel studies may report overlapping data.

supplement.evidence: No supplementary tables referenced for the frequency data; Tables 1 and 2 contain the counts.

assessment.summary: Primary molecular study of P. falciparum resistance markers (dhfr, dhps, pfcrt-76, pfmdr1-86) in clinical malaria episodes among Mozambican infants in an IPTi-SP trial in Manhiça. Extractable frequencies with denominators are given in Tables 1 and 2 at a single sub-national site. However, no explicit calendar collection dates are provided and samples span the trial's full follow-up period, so the <=3-year temporal resolution cannot be confirmed. Duplicate risk is high because samples come from the published IPTi trial cohort.
exclusion_reasons: No usable collection year/window reported; cannot confirm frequencies tie to a collection window of at most three years.
